title: campanula rotundifolia subsp. balcanica
intro: Campanula rotundifolia subsp. balcanica, also known as the Balkan bellflower, is a subspecies of flowering plant belonging to the Campanula genus. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The Balkan bellflower is believed to offer health benefits such as reducing fever, treating respiratory issues, and providing relief from digestive problems. It can be prepared as an herbal tea or tincture, and in some regions, the leaves and flowers are also used in culinary preparations, though its safety and efficacy in these uses require further research.
